core_mechanism: a non-commutative operator-algebra probability calculus in which outcomes are represented by a positive operator-valued measure on a Hilbert space, so that superposition of alternatives generates interference cross-terms that make the operations noncommutative and the outcome probabilities non-additive (violating classical additivity)
brief_reason: The abstract builds its theory from a probability operator measure and an algebra of probability operators whose entangling/composite structure produces quantum interference terms, rendering actions noncommutative and prospect probabilities non-additive.
